articles.title: Big Start: West Bengal Records 18.39% Voting in First 2 Hours
articles.description: In the second and decisive phase of voting in West Bengal, turnout has been strong since morning, with 18.39% polling recorded in the first two hours (till 9 AM) across 142 seats. Voting began at 7 AM and is progressing at a brisk pace, with expectations of high overall turnout similar to previous phases. Several prominent leaders, including Suvendu Adhikari, Abhishek Banerjee, Mahua Moitra, and Mithun Chakraborty, have already cast their votes. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee is also seen inspecting polling booths. The TMC has alleged irregularities, accusing central forces of bias, while incidents of violence and EVM-related complaints have been reported from areas like Howrah and Nadia. Security forces have intervened to control unrest in some locations. Analysts note that higher voter turnout may be influenced by electoral roll concerns (SIR), prompting more people to ensure their names remain active in voter lists. The final turnout is expected to remain high as polling continues.

articles.title: Trump and King Charles related? Viral genealogy sparks buzz
articles.description: Trump King Charles: A report traces a shared ancestor in the Earl of Lennox, linking the UK monarch and US president as distant cousins, while historians flag gaps in records and note the long UK-US 'special relationship'. Mail details lineage paths.

articles.title: Why are oil prices down now, and will Brent, US WTI crude futures continue to drop or rise again? Oil slips after UAE exits OPEC and Strait tensions continue
articles.description: Why are oil prices down now, and will Brent, US WTI crude futures continue to drop or rise again? Oil prices eased after a long rally as markets reacted to the exit of OPEC by the United Arab Emirates. Supply risks remain due to the conflict involving Iran and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Investors are now watching supply, inventories, and future geopolitical moves.

articles.title: Breaking: PM Modi Visits Kashi Vishwanath Temple Amid High Political Buzz
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited the Kashi Vishwanath Temple in Varanasi on Monday, where he offered prayers and participated in traditional rituals amid significant public attention. The visit featured symbolic visuals including devotional chanting, drum beats, and ceremonial elements that marked a strong cultural setting around the event. Supporters viewed the imagery of spiritual instruments such as the damru and trishul as reinforcing a broader narrative of cultural identity and tradition. The visit also comes at a politically sensitive time, with West Bengal witnessing intense electoral activity and heightened public engagement during the voting phases. Political observers note that such high-visibility religious visits often generate discussion around symbolism and messaging, especially when elections are underway in key states. While official statements focus on spiritual engagement and cultural heritage, the visuals from the temple visit quickly circulated across media platforms and social channels. The event has drawn attention not only for its religious significance but also for its perceived political undertones in the broader national context. In West Bengal, where electoral competition remains strong, every major national-level appearance is closely analyzed for potential influence on voter sentiment. Security arrangements around the temple were heightened due to the Prime Minister’s visit, ensuring smooth conduct of rituals and crowd management. Officials confirmed that the visit proceeded without disruption, and devotees gathered in large numbers to witness the ceremonial activities. The visuals of traditional instruments and temple rituals continue to circulate widely, keeping the visit in public discussion throughout the day. Such events often become part of broader political discourse, especially during election periods, where symbolism plays a key role in narrative building. The visit is being followed closely by both supporters and critics across the political spectrum. Further reactions are expected today globally.

articles.title: Iran-Israel War LIVE Updates: Israel Strikes South Lebanon Despite Ceasefire; Hezbollah Continues Drone Strikes
articles.description: Iran War News Live Updates: Follow real-time developments as the US-Israel conflict with Iran unfolds amid escalating political rhetoric, stalled diplomacy and continued military exchanges across the Middle East. Track the latest strategic decisions, diplomatic efforts, regional clashes and economic impacts affecting global energy markets and security dynamics. US Raises Alarm Over Iran’s Nuclear Intentions: US Senator Marco Rubio has warned that Iran is “sprinting towards” acquiring nuclear weapons capability, stressing that any future agreement must prevent Tehran from advancing its programme. He also accused Iran of potentially using negotiations to delay decisive action. Trump Rejects Iran Proposal, Signals Pressure: US President Donald Trump has reportedly rejected Iran’s proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz while delaying nuclear talks. He has also claimed that Iran is in a “state of collapse” and has urged Tehran to move quickly to resolve tensions, including lifting restrictions impacting maritime trade. Domestic And Global Political Pressure Builds: In Washington, lawmakers are pushing War Powers resolutions to limit further military escalation without congressional approval, highlighting divisions over the conflict. In Europe, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has criticised the US approach, warning of a lack of clear strategy and growing geopolitical and economic fallout. Diplomatic Channels Remain Fragile: Despite tensions, diplomatic efforts continue through intermediaries, including Pakistan. Iran has defended its position at the United Nations, accusing the US and Israel of aggression while proposing interim arrangements linked to maritime access in the Strait of Hormuz. Gulf And Regional Reactions Intensify: Gulf leaders meeting in Saudi Arabia have called on Iran to take responsibility and rebuild trust after what they described as destabilising actions. The regional bloc’s stance reflects growing concern over security and economic disruption. Lebanon Front Sees Renewed Violence: Clashes between Israel and Hezbollah continue in southern Lebanon, with drone attacks wounding Israeli soldiers and retaliatory airstrikes causing casualties. Israel has been accused of killing emergency workers, an incident condemned by Lebanese President Joseph Aoun as a “war crime”. Hezbollah Hardens Position: Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem has rejected negotiations involving Israel and demanded a full Israeli withdrawal from Lebanese territory, the release of prisoners and reconstruction support, underscoring the risk of prolonged conflict on the northern front. Energy And Economic Shockwaves Expand: The conflict continues to disrupt global energy dynamics. In a significant development, the UAE has announced it will exit OPEC after nearly six decades, signalling a potential shift in global oil alliances amid ongoing instability. Strait Of Hormuz Remains Central To Crisis: The strategic waterway remains a focal point of both military and diplomatic manoeuvring. Iran has indicated conditional willingness to reopen it, while global markets remain sensitive to any disruption in this critical energy corridor. Conflict Remains At Critical Inflection Point: With no clear diplomatic breakthrough, continued military exchanges and rising global concern, the war remains at a highly volatile stage. The coming days will be crucial in determining whether negotiations gain traction or tensions escalate further.

articles.title: AI-based traffic system to curb violations and accidents in state
articles.description: In an ambitious move to tackle traffic woes, Bihar is rolling out a cutting-edge Intelligent Traffic Management System. This innovative initiative will harness AI technology, Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) cameras, and smart signaling to alleviate congestion and reduce accidents. With the deployment of over 500 cameras to monitor infractions, the system will issue electronic fines to encourage rule adherence.

Featuring a 100-litre tank, 20-meter air throw, and antifungal honeycomb pads, it promises efficient cooling even in challenging summers. Its hard water-resistant pump and user-friendly features ensure durability and ease of use, backed by a solid warranty and extensive service network.

articles.title: 'Topper of my class, still no job': Graduate spent over Rs 1 crore on UK degree, applied to 500 jobs, got fewer than 20 interviews
articles.description: UK graduate job crisis: A 21-year-old graduate’s struggle to find a job in the UK has gone viral, highlighting the growing challenges in the graduate job market. Khaled Sharif, a top-performing student from Kingston University, has applied to nearly 500 jobs but received fewer than 20 interview calls. Despite spending over ₹1 crore on his education, he remains unemployed and has expanded his search beyond his field. He attributes the situation to an oversupply of graduates, reduced hiring, and increasing competition, while also suspecting that misconceptions about his work eligibility may be affecting his chances.
